kept.text:
DoorDash driver who allegedly recorded a naked customer on his couch before claiming to be the victim has been indicted by an Oswego County Grand Jury.

23-year-old Olivia Henderson posted a viral TikTok of a man on his own couch before crying that DoorDash fired her.

Court documents say Henderson, who has pleaded not guilty, degraded the man by recording “intimate parts of such person at a place and time when such person has a reasonable expectation of privacy, without such person’s knowledge or consent.”

Talk about a backfire.

kept.text:
This employee got a call about a DoorDash order that was already delivered. The customer ordered a steak and cheese, a meatball, and a pepperoni and salami sandwich.

When another employee made it, the store was out of steak and mayo so she just made another meatball sandwich without consulting with the customer first. The customer is upset and says that if they were out of steak then they should have called and let her know so she could get something else instead of having the decision made for her.

The employee defended the other employee and claims they aren’t allowed to call the customer and that the situation was appropriate. When the customer gets mad she then tells her to come down to the store as if to provoke a confrontation.  Was the customer wrong for ordering from a third party site that doesn’t show their inventory in real time or should the employee have let the door dasher know so they could call the customer?

kept.text:
You drove for DoorDash on Saturday. Earned $145 over five hours.

Subtract the gas. Subtract the wear on the car most drivers never run the math on. Subtract the self-employment tax that lands in April.

What's left is somewhere around $9 an hour. The customers tipped fine. The car ate it.

kept.text:
Founders pitching personalized AI agents almost always arrive at the same line about a "data moat" that gets built one user at a time. Then you ask them how they get the first 500 quality data points on me, and the answer is some version of: scrape my X, my Spotify, my DoorDash, my camera roll, my emails, my texts. I sat through dozens of these pitches when I was at a venture firm and I never bought it. 

The first reason is legal. Most of those companies do not want you scraping their data. 

The second reason is the part founders will not admit to investors. Trying to figure out who someone is from the tweets they liked at two in the morning is not very indicative of their personhood.

The actual move is to find a use case where users tell you the truth about themselves on purpose. There are not many of those, which is part of why dating is one of the first places AI personalization will work.

profile_bio.description: Stanford dropout | co-founder of Known AI

quoted_tweet.text:
DoorDash driver who allegedly recorded a naked customer on his couch before claiming to be the victim has been indicted by an Oswego County Grand Jury.

23-year-old Olivia Henderson posted a viral TikTok of a man on his own couch before crying that DoorDash fired her.

Court documents say Henderson, who has pleaded not guilty, degraded the man by recording “intimate parts of such person at a place and time when such person has a reasonable expectation of privacy, without such person’s knowledge or consent.”

Talk about a backfire.
